metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for transportation, directions, and travel

content.name: winter_vacation
content.side_e: Hánjià wǒ yào huí jiā.
content.side_f: During winter vacation I want to go home.
content.side_g: 寒假 is the winter school break (typically late Jan–Feb around Spring Festival); its counterpart is 暑假 shǔjià (summer vacation). Commonly used with 放 (fàng hánjià, 'to have/start winter vacation') and 过 (guò hánjià, 'to spend winter vacation'). It is a noun, not a verb.

content.name: airplane
content.side_e: Wǒ zuò fēijī qù Běijīng.
content.side_f: I go to Beijing by plane.
content.side_g: 飞 (fly) + 机 (machine). Measure word is 架 jià (一架飞机). To 'board' use 上飞机, to 'deplane' 下飞机. Note the pattern 坐飞机 (to travel by plane) — not 开飞机, which means 'to pilot a plane.'

content.name: ticket
content.side_e: Wǒ mǎi le liǎng zhāng piào.
content.side_f: I bought two tickets.
content.side_g: 票 is a bound noun usually compounded: 飞机票 (plane ticket), 电影票 (movie ticket), 火车票 (train ticket). Measure word is 张 zhāng (for flat objects). 'To buy a ticket' = 买票; 'to book a ticket' = 订票 dìngpiào.

content.name: airport
content.side_e: Fēijīchǎng lí jiā hěn yuǎn.
content.side_f: The airport is far from home.
content.side_g: Literally 'airplane-field.' The modern/formal equivalent is 机场 jīchǎng, which is more common in signage and news; 飞机场 is the fuller textbook form. Use 去机场 or 到机场 to say 'go to the airport.'

content.name: to_travel_by
content.side_e: Wǒmen zuò dìtiě qù xuéxiào.
content.side_f: We take the subway to school.
content.side_g: 坐 originally means 'to sit' but functions as the default verb for riding passenger transport: 坐车, 坐飞机, 坐船, 坐火车. Contrast with 骑 qí (ride straddling: 骑自行车/马) and 开 (drive oneself). 坐下 = 'sit down.'

content.name: bus
content.side_e: Gōnggòng qìchē tài màn le.
content.side_f: The (public) bus is too slow.
content.side_g: Literally 'public gas-vehicle.' In mainland speech it is often shortened to 公交车 gōngjiāochē or just 公车 gōngchē; in Taiwan 公车 is standard. Classifier is 辆 liàng. Pair with 坐 (坐公共汽车) for 'take the bus.'

content.name: or
content.side_e: Zuò chē huòzhě zǒu lù dōu xíng.
content.side_f: By car or on foot, either is fine.
content.side_g: 或者 is the declarative 'or' used in statements and offers ('A or B, either works'). Do NOT use it in yes/no questions — use 还是 háishi there (你坐车还是走路？). 或者…或者… can also mean 'either…or…' listing alternatives.

content.name: subway
content.side_e: Dìtiě bǐ gōnggòng qìchē kuài.
content.side_f: The subway is faster than the bus.
content.side_g: Literally 'underground iron.' Measure word 条 tiáo for lines (一条地铁线) or 趟 tàng for a single run. Colors name lines: 红线, 绿线, 蓝线. Related terms: 地铁站 (subway station), 换乘 huànchéng (transfer).

content.name: walk
content.side_a: To go by way of; to walk
content.side_e: Wǒ měitiān zǒu lù qù xuéxiào.
content.side_f: I walk to school every day.
content.side_g: 走 in modern Mandarin primarily means 'walk / go on foot,' NOT 'run' (that's 跑 pǎo — opposite of Classical Chinese). 走 also means 'to leave' (我先走了 = 'I'm leaving first'). 走路 = walk as an activity; 走 + location = go by way of.

content.name: first
content.side_e: Nǐ xiān zuò chē, ránhòu zǒu lù.
content.side_f: First take the bus, then walk.
content.side_g: 先 is an adverb and must precede the verb (我先去), never stand alone as an answer. Commonly paired: 先…然后… or 先…再 zài… (先吃饭再去). 你先请 = 'after you / you first' (polite).

content.name: stop_measure_word
content.side_a: (Measure word for stops of bus, train, etc.)
content.side_e: Zuò sān zhàn xià chē.
content.side_f: Ride three stops and get off.
content.side_g: 站 is both the noun 'station/stop' (火车站, 地铁站) and the measure word for counting stops (五站 = five stops). It can also mean 'to stand' as a verb (站起来 = stand up). Context disambiguates.

content.name: get_off
content.side_a: To get off (a bus, train, etc.)
content.side_e: Dào le xià yí zhàn wǒ xià chē.
content.side_f: At the next stop I'll get off.
content.side_g: 下车 is a separable verb-object compound: you can insert aspect/measure between them (下了车, 下一下车). Its opposite 上车 = 'to board.' Use 在…下车 for 'get off at…': 在第三站下车.

content.name: then
content.side_e: Xiān chī fàn, ránhòu zuò chē qù.
content.side_f: First eat, then take the bus there.
content.side_g: 然后 links sequential events ('after that / then') and is usually preceded by 先. It is time-sequential, unlike 所以 (causal 'therefore') or 而且 (additive 'moreover'). In casual speech 然后 is also a filler, like English 'and then… um…'.

content.name: green
content.side_e: Lǜ xiàn hěn kuài.
content.side_f: The green line is fast.
content.side_g: 绿 describes the color green broadly; for the plant/leaf green 青 qīng is more literary. Note pronunciation: mainland standard is lǜ. Common compounds: 绿茶 (green tea), 绿灯 (green light), 绿色 (green-colored).

content.name: line
content.side_e: Zuò lǜ xiàn dào zuìhòu yí zhàn.
content.side_f: Take the green line to the last stop.
content.side_g: 线 means 'line/thread/route.' For subway/bus routes it combines with colors (红线) or numbers (一号线 yī hào xiàn = Line 1 — the more official form in mainland cities). Also seen in 路线 lùxiàn (route), 在线 zàixiàn (online), 电线 (electric wire).

content.name: final
content.side_e: Zuìhòu wǒmen dǎ chē huí jiā.
content.side_f: In the end we took a taxi home.
content.side_g: 最后 means 'finally/last' in sequence or position (最后一站 = last stop). Distinguish from 最近 zuìjìn (recently) and 终于 zhōngyú ('at long last,' emphasizing expectation fulfilled). As a discourse marker: 最后,… = 'Lastly,…' in enumerations.

content.name: blue
content.side_e: Lán xiàn bǐ lǜ xiàn máfan.
content.side_f: The blue line is more of a hassle than the green line.
content.side_g: 蓝 = blue; 蓝色 is the usual color-noun form. In classical contexts 青 can cover blue/green/black. Common compounds: 蓝天 (blue sky), 蓝牙 (bluetooth), 蓝色 (the color blue).

content.name: troublesome
content.side_e: Zuò dìtiě tài máfan le.
content.side_f: Taking the subway is too much hassle.
content.side_g: 麻烦 is adjective ('troublesome') AND verb ('to bother someone'): 麻烦你 = 'may I trouble you (to…).' The second syllable 烦 is neutral tone. Polite request frame: 麻烦你 + verb phrase. Noun usage: 有麻烦了 = 'in trouble.'

content.name: take_taxi
content.side_e: Wǒmen dǎ chē qù fēijīchǎng ba.
content.side_f: Let's take a taxi to the airport.
content.side_g: 打车 literally 'hit a car' is the colloquial verb for hailing a taxi (or nowadays a ride-share 打的 dǎ dī / 打车). Separable: 打了一辆车. Synonyms: 叫出租车 (call a taxi), 叫车 (call/order a car). More colloquial than 坐出租汽车.

content.name: taxi
content.side_e: Chūzū qìchē hěn guì.
content.side_f: Taxis are expensive.
content.side_g: Literally 'rent-out gas-vehicle.' Often shortened to 出租车 chūzūchē; in Beijing speech 的士 dīshì (from Cantonese/HK) is also common, giving the verb 打的 dǎdī. Measure word 辆 liàng.

content.name: drive
content.side_e: Wǒ bàba kāi chē sòng wǒ.
content.side_f: My dad drives me (there).
content.side_g: 开 literally 'open/operate' is used for driving vehicles you steer: 开车 (drive a car), 开飞机 (pilot a plane), 开船 (sail a ship). Separable: 开了三个小时的车. Contrast 坐 (ride as passenger) and 骑 (ride astride).

content.name: see_off
content.side_a: To see off or out; to take (someone somewhere)
content.side_e: Míngtiān wǒ sòng nǐ qù fēijīchǎng.
content.side_f: Tomorrow I'll take you to the airport (see you off).
content.side_g: 送 has three core senses: (1) see someone off / escort — 送你回家; (2) deliver or take something/someone somewhere — 送外卖; (3) give as a gift — 送礼物. Context and object decide. Opposite of 接 jiē (to pick up/meet).
